title: pedicularis diffusa subsp. elatior
intro: Pedicularis diffusa subsp. elatior, also known as tall lousewort, is a species of flowering plant in the balm family, native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Pedicularis diffusa subsp. elatior include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
